name: Intermittent Control Is Not Diluted Control: A Switching Effect in Artificial Agency
summary: arXiv:2607.17432v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Adaptive agents do not always regulate under the same timing conditions. Sometimes stabilization can begin before a disturbance has fully entered the...
description: arXiv:2607.17432v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Adaptive agents do not always regulate under the same timing conditions. Sometimes stabilization can begin before a disturbance has fully entered the internal state; at other times the agent can only recover after disruption has taken hold. A simple expectation is that an agent moving between these conditions should behave like a weighted average...
author: Veronique Ziegler
